toolCalls.name: complete_task

args.result:
Complete feature inventory of both reference implementations in `/Users/jiayin/workspace/dev/dev/rust/truefix/thrdpty/`. Both are git-ignored subdirs (full clones). All findings below are read from actual source files.

---

# 1. QuickFIX/Go (`thrdpty/quickfix/`)

## Config keys (`config/configuration.go`) ŌĆö ALL keys, grouped as in source

**Session settings:** `BeginString`, `SenderCompID`, `SenderSubID`, `SenderLocationID`, `TargetCompID`, `TargetSubID`, `TargetLocationID`, `SessionQualifier`, `DefaultApplVerID`, `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `Weekdays`, `TimeZone` (default UTC; IANA), `TimeStampPrecision` (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS, default MILLIS), `ResetOnLogon`, `RefreshOnLogon`, `ResetOnLogout`, `ResetOnDisconnect`, `ResetSeqTime` (HH:MM:SS, seqnum reset while staying connected).

**Validation settings:** `DataDictionary`, `TransportDataDictionary`, `AppDataDictionary` (supports `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4` prefix for per-ApplVerID dicts), `RejectInvalidMessage` (default Y), `AllowUnknownMsgFields` (const name; key string `ValidateUserDefinedFields`... actually key is `"AllowUnknownMsgFields"`), `CheckUserDefinedFields` (key string `"ValidateUserDefinedFields"`, default Y), `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder` (default Y), `ValidateFieldsHaveValues` (default Y), `CheckLatency` (default Y), `MaxLatency` (default 120), `InChanCapacity` (default 1; 0 = unbuffered).

**Initiator-only:** `ReconnectInterval` (default 30), `LogoutTimeout` (default 2), `LogonTimeout` (default 10), `HeartBtInt`, `SocketConnectHost` (+ `SocketConnectHost<n>` failover), `SocketConnectPort` (+ `SocketConnectPort<n>` failover), `SocketTimeout` (TLS handshake; go Duration), `ProxyType`, `ProxyHost`, `ProxyPort`, `ProxyUser`, `ProxyPassword`.

**Acceptor-only:** `SocketAcceptHost`, `SocketAcceptPort`, `HeartBtIntOverride` (default N), `UseTCPProxy` (HAProxy/ELB PROXY proto, default N), `DynamicSessions` (default N), `DynamicQualifier` (default N).

**Security/TLS:** `SocketPrivateKeyFile`, `SocketCertificateFile`, `SocketCAFile`, `SocketPrivateKeyBytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketCertificateBytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketCABytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketInsecureSkipVerify`, `SocketServerName` (SNI), `SocketMinimumTLSVersion` (SSL30/TLS10/TLS11/TLS12; default TLS12), `SocketUseSSL`.

**Logging:** `FileLogPath`, `SQLLogDriver`, `SQLLogDataSourceName`, `SQLLogConnMaxLifetime`, `MongoLogConnection`, `MongoLogDatabase`, `MongoLogReplicaSet`.

**Storage:** `PersistMessages` (default Y), `FileStorePath`, `FileStoreSync` (default Y), `SQLStoreDriver`, `SQLStoreDataSourceName`, `SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime`, `SQLStoreMessagesTableName` (default "messages"), `SQLStoreSessionsTableName` (default "sessions"), `MongoStoreConnection`, `MongoStoreDatabase`, `MongoStoreReplicaSet`.

**Misc:** `ResendRequestChunkSize` (default 0 = no chunking), `EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed` (tag 369), `EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum` (tag 789; not for <FIX.4.4).

## `session_factory.go` ŌĆö session options
Reads validator settings (ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields). Builds `Validator` (default w/o dictionaries; DataDictionary / TransportDataDictionary+AppDataDictionary for FIXT). FIXT requires `DefaultApplVerID` (maps BeginStringŌåÆApplVerID: FIX40=2ŌĆ”FIX5.0SP2=9). Reads ResetOnLogon/RefreshOnLogon/ResetOnLogout/ResetOnDisconnect/EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed/EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum/CheckLatency/MaxLatency/ResendRequestChunkSize. **StartTime/EndTime + TimeZone + StartDay/EndDay OR Weekdays** (mutually exclusive) ŌåÆ `internal.TimeRange`. **`ResetSeqTime`** ŌåÆ `s.EnableResetSeqTime=true`, `s.ResetSeqTime` parsed HH:MM:SS in TimeZone. **`TimeStampPrecision`** ŌåÆ Seconds/Millis/Micros/Nanos. **`InChanCapacity`** (default 1, must be Ōēź0). Initiator-only: ReconnectInterval (default 30s), LogoutTimeout (default 2s), LogonTimeout (default 10s), `configureSocketConnectAddress` collects all `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` into `session.SocketConnectAddress []string`. **`HeartBtIntOverride`**: acceptor uses config HeartBtInt only if override=Y; initiator always requires HeartBtInt.

## `acceptor.go`
`ConnectionValidator` interface: `Validate(netConn net.Conn, session SessionID) error` ŌĆö for custom auth (mTLS, IP whitelisting). `SetConnectionValidator`/`SetTLSConfig`/`SetNewListenerCallback`. **DynamicSessions**: creates sessions on-the-fly via `dynamicSessionsLoop` goroutine, registers/unregisters dynamically. **DynamicQualifier**: appends an incrementing counter as Qualifier to allow same SenderCompID. `UseTCPProxy` wraps listeners in `proxyproto.Listener` (HAProxy/ELB). Validates incoming logon BeginString/SenderCompID/TargetCompID (+Sub/Location), checks expected local port, runs connectionValidator, looks up session. Reads `SocketAcceptPort` per-session or global.

## `initiator.go` ŌĆö failover/multi-endpoint
**YES, failover/multi-endpoint**: `session.SocketConnectAddress` is a list; `address := session.SocketConnectAddress[connectionAttempt % len(...)]` cycles through all configured `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` on each reconnect. Per-session goroutine; `waitForInSessionTime` (respects SessionTime) ŌåÆ dial via `proxy.ContextDialer` (SOCKS) ŌåÆ optional TLS handshake with auto-derived ServerName from address ŌåÆ `session.connect` + readLoop/writeLoop. Reconnect loop with `ReconnectInterval`, cancellable dial via context on stop.

## `dialer.go` ŌĆö SOCKS proxy
**YES, SOCKS5**: `ProxyType=socks` ŌåÆ `proxy.SOCKS5("tcp", host:port, proxyAuth, stdDialer)`. Supports `ProxyUser`/`ProxyPassword` auth. Falls back to plain `net.Dialer` if no ProxyType. `SocketTimeout` sets dialer timeout.

## `tls.go` ŌĆö TLS options
`loadTLSConfig`: `SocketUseSSL` (allow TLS w/o client certs), `SocketServerName`, `SocketInsecureSkipVerify`. Certs from files (`SocketPrivateKeyFile`+`SocketCertificateFile` via `tls.LoadX509KeyPair`) **OR raw PEM bytes** (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`+`SocketCertificateBytes` via `tls.X509KeyPair`). CA from file (`SocketCAFile`) **or raw bytes** (`SocketCABytes`) ŌåÆ RootCAs+ClientCAs. Default `ClientAuth = RequireAndVerifyClientCert` (unless SocketUseSSL). **Min version** configurable (`setMinVersionExplicit`): SSL30/TLS10/TLS11/TLS12; default TLS12 via `defaultTLSConfig` (also PreferServerCipherSuites, CurveP256 only).

## Stores (`store/`): file, memory, mongo, sql
- **`store/file/file_store.go`**: **`FileStoreSync`** (default Y) controls `f.Sync()` on every write. Files: `.body`, `.header` (seqNum,offset,size CSV), `.session` (creation time), `.senderseqnums`, `.targetseqnums` (19-digit zero-padded). In-memory `MemoryStore` cache; `Refresh` repopulates. `IterateMessages` (streaming) + `GetMessages`. `SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`. Respects `DynamicSessions`.
- **`store/sql/sql_store.go`**: generic multi-DB via any `database/sql` driver. **Postgres/pgx placeholder** support (`$1,$2` via `postgresPlaceholder`); other DBs use `?`. **Configurable table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName` default "messages", `SQLStoreSessionsTableName` default "sessions"). Session key columns: beginstring, session_qualifier, sendercompid, sendersubid, senderlocid, targetcompid, targetsubid, targetlocid. `SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime`. `SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum` uses a transaction. `IterateMessages` streaming.
- **memory**, **mongo** subdirs also present.

## Logs (`log/`): composite, file, mongo, screen, sql

## `internal/time_range.go` ŌĆö scheduling
`TimeRange` with `startTime`/`endTime` (TimeOfDay HH:MM:SS), `weekdays []time.Weekday`, optional `startDay`/`endDay *time.Weekday`, `loc *time.Location`. Constructors: `NewUTCTimeRange`, `NewTimeRangeInLocation`, `NewUTCWeekRange`, `NewWeekRangeInLocation`. `IsInRange(t)` handles daily, weekday-filtered, and week-spanning ranges (incl. overnight wrap). `IsInSameRange(t1,t2)` for session-boundary reset detection. Supports **StartDay/EndDay** (week-long sessions) and **Weekdays** (specific days of week) ŌĆö mutually exclusive.

## `_test/definitions/` ŌĆö 506 files
Top-level: `fields.fmt`, `lastseqnumprocessed`, `nextexpectedseqnum`, `resendreqchunksize`, `server/`. `server/` subdirs by FIX version: **fix40, fix41, fix42, fix43, fix44, fix50, fix50sp1, fix50sp2** (8 versions). Conformance/definition test specs.

## `cmd/generate-fix/` ŌĆö codegen CLI
`generate-fix.go`: takes data-dictionary XML path(s) arg. Auto-appends FIXT11 dict for FIX50/FIX50SP1/FIX50SP2. Generates (concurrently via goroutines+templates): `tag/tag_numbers.generated.go`, `field/fields.generated.go`, `enum/enums.generated.go`, per-package `header.generated.go`+`trailer.generated.go` (except fix50* which use fixt11), and per-message `<MsgName>.generated.go` under `fixPkg/<lowercaseMsgName>/`. Internal helpers: generate.go, globals.go, helpers.go, template_helpers.go, templates.go.

---

# 2. QuickFIX/J (`thrdpty/quickfixj/`) ŌĆö version 3.0.2-SNAPSHOT

## Modules
quickfixj-base, quickfixj-core, quickfixj-dictgenerator, quickfixj-orchestration, quickfixj-messages (fix40, fix41, fix42, fix43, fix44, fix50, fix50sp1, fix50sp2, **fixlatest**, fixt11, -all), quickfixj-examples, quickfixj-all, quickfixj-distribution, quickfixj-perf-test, quickfixj-stress-test. **FIX Latest module EXISTS** (`quickfixj-messages-fixlatest`).

## `docs/threading-model.md` ŌĆö threading strategies
Uses **Apache MINA** (NIO). Two `EventHandlingStrategy` impls:
- **SingleThreadedEventHandlingStrategy** ŌåÆ `SocketAcceptor`/`SocketInitiator`, single shared `BlockingQueue`, one daemon thread "QFJ Message Processor" calls `session.next(message)`.
- **ThreadPerSessionEventHandlingStrategy** ŌåÆ `ThreadedSocketAcceptor`/`ThreadedSocketInitiator`, per-session dispatcher thread "QF/J Session dispatcher: <sessionID>".
- **Timer thread** "QFJ Timer" (daemon, `ScheduledExecutorService` every 1000ms) iterates ALL sessions calling `Session.next()` (no-arg) for heartbeats/logon/schedule/timeouts ŌĆö orthogonal to message strategy.
Queue back-pressure: fixed-capacity OR watermark-based (`QueueTrackers.newMultiSessionWatermarkTracker` / `newSingleSessionWatermarkTracker`). Custom `Executor` injectable (e.g. Java 21 virtual threads). Thread-safety note: thread-per-session requires thread-safe `Application` if sharing state.

## OSGi support (`pom.xml`)
**YES**: `org.apache.felix:maven-bundle-plugin` (5.1.9) enabled in build (`<extensions>true</extensions>`, manifest goal execution). `skipBundlePlugin` profile to disable. Other deps: MINA 2.2.7, **slf4j 2.0.18** (`slf4j-jdk14`), Sleepycat `com.sleepycat:je` 18.3.12 (optional), dom4j 2.1.4 (dictgenerator), jaxen, Saxon, fix-orchestra 1.5.4/1.7.3, JAXB 4.0.5, commons-io, Netty 4.2.15. JDK 1.8 target. Maven 3.9.12.

## `MessageCracker.java` ŌĆö typed dispatch
Reflection-based `crack(Message, SessionID)`. **`@Handler` annotation** (METHOD, RUNTIME retention). Also supports naming convention `onMessage(Message, SessionID)`. Scans public methods with 2 params (Message subclass + SessionID), builds `Map<Class<?>, Invoker>`. `RedundantHandlerException` on duplicate. Fallback `onMessage` throws `UnsupportedMessageType`. Unwraps `InvocationTargetException`.

## `ApplicationFunctionalAdapter.java` & `ApplicationExtendedFunctionalAdapter.java`
Functional/lambda-style adapters. `ApplicationFunctionalAdapter` implements `Application`: CopyOnWriteArrayList listeners for onCreate/onLogon/onLogout; BiConsumer for toAdmin; custom `FromAdminListener`/`ToAppListener`/`FromAppListener` for fromAdmin/toApp/fromApp. **Type-safe listeners** keyed by Message class via `ConcurrentMap<Class, List>`. FIFO order; fail-fast exception propagation. `ApplicationExtendedFunctionalAdapter` extends it, implements `ApplicationExtended`: `canLogonPredicates` (Predicate<SessionID>, all-match/fail-fast) and `onBeforeSessionResetListeners` (Consumer<SessionID>).

## `JmxExporter.java`
JMX MBean export for connectors. Registration behavior constants: `REGISTRATION_FAIL_ON_EXISTING` (default), `REGISTRATION_IGNORE_EXISTING`, `REGISTRATION_REPLACE_EXISTING`. Uses platform MBeanServer (`ManagementFactory.getPlatformMBeanServer`) or creates one. `register(Connector)` delegates to `ConnectorJmxExporter`. Uses SLF4J logger. Spring-inspired MBeanRegistrationSupport design.

## `DoNotSend.java`, `RejectLogon.java`, `FieldNotFound.java`
- `DoNotSend` (Exception, base): thrown to abort sending/resending a message.
- `RejectLogon` (Exception): rejects logon with forced logout + immediate disconnect. Fields: `logoutBeforeDisconnect` (default true), `sessionStatus` (SessionStatus value, default -1). Multiple constructors.
- `FieldNotFound` (Exception): public `int field`; constructor `FieldNotFound(int field)`. Note about accessing fields in correct message section.

## Store types (`quickfixj-core/.../quickfix/`)
**FileStore**, **CachedFileStore** (+ CachedFileStoreFactory), **MemoryStore** (+ MemoryStoreFactory), **JdbcStore** (+ JdbcStoreFactory, JdbcUtil, JdbcSetting, JdbcLog), **SleepycatStore** (+ SleepycatStoreFactory, Berkeley DB JE), plus **NoopStore** (+ NoopStoreFactory), InMemoryMessageQueue (+ BoundInMemoryMessageQueue), MessageStore/MessageStoreFactory interfaces.

## `FileStore.java` ŌĆö syncWrites, maxCachedMsgs, ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore
Constructor `FileStore(path, sessionID, syncWrites, maxCachedMsgs)`. **`syncWrites`**: when true, opens files with mode `"rwd"` (RandomAccessFile SYNC_OPTION 'd') and calls `headerFileOutputStream.getFD().sync()` after each header write. **`maxCachedMsgs`**: when >0, maintains in-memory `TreeMap<Long,long[]> messageIndex` (seqNumŌåÆ[offset,size]) capped at maxCachedMsgs (evicts lowest via `pollFirstEntry`); when 0, index is null (always reads header file). Files: `.body`, `.header` (binary: int seqNum, long offset, int size), `.senderseqnums`, `.targetseqnums` (writeUTF), `.session` (creation time). `FileStoreFactory`: `SETTING_FILE_STORE_MAX_CACHED_MSGS = "FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs"` (default 10000), `SETTING_FILE_STORE_SYNC`. **`ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore`** is a Session setting (not FileStore) ŌĆö see config below.

## Config options (from `Session.java` grep) ŌĆö ALL confirmed present
`CheckCompID` (`SETTING_CHECK_COMP_ID`), `NonStopSession` (`SETTING_NON_STOP_SESSION`, in DefaultSessionSchedule), `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`, `ValidateUnorderedGroupFields`, `FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter`, `RejectInvalidMessage`, `RejectMessageOnUnhandledException` (`SETTING_REJECT_MESSAGE_ON_UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION`), `ResetOnError`, `DisconnectOnError`, `DisableHeartBeatCheck`, `SendRedundantResendRequests`, `ClosedResendInterval` (`SETTING_USE_CLOSED_RESEND_INTERVAL`), `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore`, `MaxScheduledWriteRequests` (`SETTING_MAX_SCHEDULED_WRITE_REQUESTS`), `LogonTag` (`SETTING_LOGON_TAG` ŌĆö tag=value pairs, e.g. LogonTag=553=user, LogonTag1=554=password; List<StringField>), `TimeStampPrecision` (`SETTING_TIMESTAMP_PRECISION`, UtcTimestampPrecision enum, MILLIS default, SECONDS for <FIX.4.2), `ContinueInitializationOnError` (`SETTING_CONTINUE_INIT_ON_ERROR` in SessionFactory). Session constructor also takes: checkLatency/maxLatency, refreshOnLogon, checkCompID, logonIntervals, allowedRemoteAddresses (Set<InetAddress>), requiresOrigSendingTime, validateChecksum, heartBeatTimeoutMultiplier, rejectGarbledMessage. `DefaultSessionSchedule`/`SessionSchedule` interface has `isNonStopSession()`.

## SLF4J integration
**YES**: slf4j 2.0.18, `slf4j-jdk14` binding in dependencyManagement. JmxExporter uses `org.slf4j.Logger`. Pervasive logging via SLF4J throughout.

## `dictgenerator/Generator.java`
`org.quickfixj.dictgenerator.Generator` (QFJ-483). Takes FPL 2008/09 repository dir + major/minor. `generate()` produces `session.xml` (admin), `application.xml`, and `merged.xml` (for <FIX5.0). Uses dom4j. Note: only works with FPL 2008/09 repository files. (FIX Latest generation uses the separate orchestra tools / quickfixj-orchestration module + `quickfixj-from-fix-orchestra-*-maven-plugin`.)

## Examples (`quickfixj-examples/`)
- **executor**: `Application.java`, `Executor.java`, `MarketDataProvider.java`.
- **banzai**: `Banzai.java`, `BanzaiApplication.java`, `Order.java`, `OrderTableModel.java`, `ExecutionTableModel.java`, `OrderSide/OrderTIF/OrderType`, `MarketDataProvider`, `ui/` (Swing GUI), `DoubleNumberTextField`/`IntegerNumberTextField`, `LogonEvent`, `TwoWayMap`.
- **ordermatch**: `Application.java`, `Main.java`, `Market.java`, `Order.java`, `OrderMatcher.java`, `IdGenerator.java`.

## Cross-cutting comparison notes
- Both support FIX 4.0ŌĆō4.4, 5.0/SP1/SP2, FIXT1.1; QFJ additionally has **FIX Latest** module (Go does not).
- Go stores: file/memory/sql/mongo. QFJ stores: file/cachedfile/memory/jdbc/sleepycat(Berkeley)/noop + in-memory message queues.
- Both have file-store sync option (Go `FileStoreSync`, QFJ `syncWrites`/`FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs`).
- Both support TLS (Go richer raw-bytes+min-version; QFJ via MINA SSL), SOCKS proxy (Go only via `golang.org/x/net/proxy`), TCP PROXY proto (Go `UseTCPProxy`).
- Both support multi-endpoint failover (Go `SocketConnectHost<n>` modulo cycling; QFJ `SocketConnectHost<n>` via initiator reconnect).
- Go uses config-driven validation; QFJ has richer session-level knobs (NonStopSession, ResetOnError, DisconnectOnError, DisableHeartBeatCheck, SendRedundantResendRequests, ClosedResendInterval, ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore, RejectMessageOnUnhandledException, MaxScheduledWriteRequests, LogonTag, CheckCompID, ValidateUnorderedGroupFields, FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter, ContinueInitializationOnError, heartBeatTimeoutMultiplier, allowedRemoteAddresses, validateChecksum).
- QFJ: OSGi bundles, SLF4J, JMX, MINA NIO, functional Application adapters, `@Handler` annotation cracking, dynamic FIX Latest codegen via Orchestra.

# 1. QuickFIX/Go (`thrdpty/quickfix/`)

## Config keys (`config/configuration.go`) ŌĆö ALL keys, grouped as in source

**Session settings:** `BeginString`, `SenderCompID`, `SenderSubID`, `SenderLocationID`, `TargetCompID`, `TargetSubID`, `TargetLocationID`, `SessionQualifier`, `DefaultApplVerID`, `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `Weekdays`, `TimeZone` (default UTC; IANA), `TimeStampPrecision` (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS, default MILLIS), `ResetOnLogon`, `RefreshOnLogon`, `ResetOnLogout`, `ResetOnDisconnect`, `ResetSeqTime` (HH:MM:SS, seqnum reset while staying connected).

**Validation settings:** `DataDictionary`, `TransportDataDictionary`, `AppDataDictionary` (supports `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4` prefix for per-ApplVerID dicts), `RejectInvalidMessage` (default Y), `AllowUnknownMsgFields` (const name; key string `ValidateUserDefinedFields`... actually key is `"AllowUnknownMsgFields"`), `CheckUserDefinedFields` (key string `"ValidateUserDefinedFields"`, default Y), `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder` (default Y), `ValidateFieldsHaveValues` (default Y), `CheckLatency` (default Y), `MaxLatency` (default 120), `InChanCapacity` (default 1; 0 = unbuffered).

**Initiator-only:** `ReconnectInterval` (default 30), `LogoutTimeout` (default 2), `LogonTimeout` (default 10), `HeartBtInt`, `SocketConnectHost` (+ `SocketConnectHost<n>` failover), `SocketConnectPort` (+ `SocketConnectPort<n>` failover), `SocketTimeout` (TLS handshake; go Duration), `ProxyType`, `ProxyHost`, `ProxyPort`, `ProxyUser`, `ProxyPassword`.

**Acceptor-only:** `SocketAcceptHost`, `SocketAcceptPort`, `HeartBtIntOverride` (default N), `UseTCPProxy` (HAProxy/ELB PROXY proto, default N), `DynamicSessions` (default N), `DynamicQualifier` (default N).

**Security/TLS:** `SocketPrivateKeyFile`, `SocketCertificateFile`, `SocketCAFile`, `SocketPrivateKeyBytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketCertificateBytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketCABytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketInsecureSkipVerify`, `SocketServerName` (SNI), `SocketMinimumTLSVersion` (SSL30/TLS10/TLS11/TLS12; default TLS12), `SocketUseSSL`.

**Logging:** `FileLogPath`, `SQLLogDriver`, `SQLLogDataSourceName`, `SQLLogConnMaxLifetime`, `MongoLogConnection`, `MongoLogDatabase`, `MongoLogReplicaSet`.

**Storage:** `PersistMessages` (default Y), `FileStorePath`, `FileStoreSync` (default Y), `SQLStoreDriver`, `SQLStoreDataSourceName`, `SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime`, `SQLStoreMessagesTableName` (default "messages"), `SQLStoreSessionsTableName` (default "sessions"), `MongoStoreConnection`, `MongoStoreDatabase`, `MongoStoreReplicaSet`.

**Misc:** `ResendRequestChunkSize` (default 0 = no chunking), `EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed` (tag 369), `EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum` (tag 789; not for <FIX.4.4).

## `session_factory.go` ŌĆö session options
Reads validator settings (ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields). Builds `Validator` (default w/o dictionaries; DataDictionary / TransportDataDictionary+AppDataDictionary for FIXT). FIXT requires `DefaultApplVerID` (maps BeginStringŌåÆApplVerID: FIX40=2ŌĆ”FIX5.0SP2=9). Reads ResetOnLogon/RefreshOnLogon/ResetOnLogout/ResetOnDisconnect/EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed/EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum/CheckLatency/MaxLatency/ResendRequestChunkSize. **StartTime/EndTime + TimeZone + StartDay/EndDay OR Weekdays** (mutually exclusive) ŌåÆ `internal.TimeRange`. **`ResetSeqTime`** ŌåÆ `s.EnableResetSeqTime=true`, `s.ResetSeqTime` parsed HH:MM:SS in TimeZone. **`TimeStampPrecision`** ŌåÆ Seconds/Millis/Micros/Nanos. **`InChanCapacity`** (default 1, must be Ōēź0). Initiator-only: ReconnectInterval (default 30s), LogoutTimeout (default 2s), LogonTimeout (default 10s), `configureSocketConnectAddress` collects all `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` into `session.SocketConnectAddress []string`. **`HeartBtIntOverride`**: acceptor uses config HeartBtInt only if override=Y; initiator always requires HeartBtInt.

## `acceptor.go`
`ConnectionValidator` interface: `Validate(netConn net.Conn, session SessionID) error` ŌĆö for custom auth (mTLS, IP whitelisting). `SetConnectionValidator`/`SetTLSConfig`/`SetNewListenerCallback`. **DynamicSessions**: creates sessions on-the-fly via `dynamicSessionsLoop` goroutine, registers/unregisters dynamically. **DynamicQualifier**: appends an incrementing counter as Qualifier to allow same SenderCompID. `UseTCPProxy` wraps listeners in `proxyproto.Listener` (HAProxy/ELB). Validates incoming logon BeginString/SenderCompID/TargetCompID (+Sub/Location), checks expected local port, runs connectionValidator, looks up session. Reads `SocketAcceptPort` per-session or global.

## `initiator.go` ŌĆö failover/multi-endpoint
**YES, failover/multi-endpoint**: `session.SocketConnectAddress` is a list; `address := session.SocketConnectAddress[connectionAttempt % len(...)]` cycles through all configured `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` on each reconnect. Per-session goroutine; `waitForInSessionTime` (respects SessionTime) ŌåÆ dial via `proxy.ContextDialer` (SOCKS) ŌåÆ optional TLS handshake with auto-derived ServerName from address ŌåÆ `session.connect` + readLoop/writeLoop. Reconnect loop with `ReconnectInterval`, cancellable dial via context on stop.

## `dialer.go` ŌĆö SOCKS proxy
**YES, SOCKS5**: `ProxyType=socks` ŌåÆ `proxy.SOCKS5("tcp", host:port, proxyAuth, stdDialer)`. Supports `ProxyUser`/`ProxyPassword` auth. Falls back to plain `net.Dialer` if no ProxyType. `SocketTimeout` sets dialer timeout.

## `tls.go` ŌĆö TLS options
`loadTLSConfig`: `SocketUseSSL` (allow TLS w/o client certs), `SocketServerName`, `SocketInsecureSkipVerify`. Certs from files (`SocketPrivateKeyFile`+`SocketCertificateFile` via `tls.LoadX509KeyPair`) **OR raw PEM bytes** (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`+`SocketCertificateBytes` via `tls.X509KeyPair`). CA from file (`SocketCAFile`) **or raw bytes** (`SocketCABytes`) ŌåÆ RootCAs+ClientCAs. Default `ClientAuth = RequireAndVerifyClientCert` (unless SocketUseSSL). **Min version** configurable (`setMinVersionExplicit`): SSL30/TLS10/TLS11/TLS12; default TLS12 via `defaultTLSConfig` (also PreferServerCipherSuites, CurveP256 only).

## Stores (`store/`): file, memory, mongo, sql
- **`store/file/file_store.go`**: **`FileStoreSync`** (default Y) controls `f.Sync()` on every write. Files: `.body`, `.header` (seqNum,offset,size CSV), `.session` (creation time), `.senderseqnums`, `.targetseqnums` (19-digit zero-padded). In-memory `MemoryStore` cache; `Refresh` repopulates. `IterateMessages` (streaming) + `GetMessages`. `SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`. Respects `DynamicSessions`.
- **`store/sql/sql_store.go`**: generic multi-DB via any `database/sql` driver. **Postgres/pgx placeholder** support (`$1,$2` via `postgresPlaceholder`); other DBs use `?`. **Configurable table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName` default "messages", `SQLStoreSessionsTableName` default "sessions"). Session key columns: beginstring, session_qualifier, sendercompid, sendersubid, senderlocid, targetcompid, targetsubid, targetlocid. `SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime`. `SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum` uses a transaction. `IterateMessages` streaming.
- **memory**, **mongo** subdirs also present.

## Logs (`log/`): composite, file, mongo, screen, sql

## `internal/time_range.go` ŌĆö scheduling
`TimeRange` with `startTime`/`endTime` (TimeOfDay HH:MM:SS), `weekdays []time.Weekday`, optional `startDay`/`endDay *time.Weekday`, `loc *time.Location`. Constructors: `NewUTCTimeRange`, `NewTimeRangeInLocation`, `NewUTCWeekRange`, `NewWeekRangeInLocation`. `IsInRange(t)` handles daily, weekday-filtered, and week-spanning ranges (incl. overnight wrap). `IsInSameRange(t1,t2)` for session-boundary reset detection. Supports **StartDay/EndDay** (week-long sessions) and **Weekdays** (specific days of week) ŌĆö mutually exclusive.

## `_test/definitions/` ŌĆö 506 files
Top-level: `fields.fmt`, `lastseqnumprocessed`, `nextexpectedseqnum`, `resendreqchunksize`, `server/`. `server/` subdirs by FIX version: **fix40, fix41, fix42, fix43, fix44, fix50, fix50sp1, fix50sp2** (8 versions). Conformance/definition test specs.

## `cmd/generate-fix/` ŌĆö codegen CLI
`generate-fix.go`: takes data-dictionary XML path(s) arg. Auto-appends FIXT11 dict for FIX50/FIX50SP1/FIX50SP2. Generates (concurrently via goroutines+templates): `tag/tag_numbers.generated.go`, `field/fields.generated.go`, `enum/enums.generated.go`, per-package `header.generated.go`+`trailer.generated.go` (except fix50* which use fixt11), and per-message `<MsgName>.generated.go` under `fixPkg/<lowercaseMsgName>/`. Internal helpers: generate.go, globals.go, helpers.go, template_helpers.go, templates.go.

## `docs/threading-model.md` ŌĆö threading strategies
Uses **Apache MINA** (NIO). Two `EventHandlingStrategy` impls:
- **SingleThreadedEventHandlingStrategy** ŌåÆ `SocketAcceptor`/`SocketInitiator`, single shared `BlockingQueue`, one daemon thread "QFJ Message Processor" calls `session.next(message)`.
- **ThreadPerSessionEventHandlingStrategy** ŌåÆ `ThreadedSocketAcceptor`/`ThreadedSocketInitiator`, per-session dispatcher thread "QF/J Session dispatcher: <sessionID>".
- **Timer thread** "QFJ Timer" (daemon, `ScheduledExecutorService` every 1000ms) iterates ALL sessions calling `Session.next()` (no-arg) for heartbeats/logon/schedule/timeouts ŌĆö orthogonal to message strategy.
Queue back-pressure: fixed-capacity OR watermark-based (`QueueTrackers.newMultiSessionWatermarkTracker` / `newSingleSessionWatermarkTracker`). Custom `Executor` injectable (e.g. Java 21 virtual threads). Thread-safety note: thread-per-session requires thread-safe `Application` if sharing state.

# 1. QuickFIX/Go (`thrdpty/quickfix/`)

## Config keys (`config/configuration.go`) ŌĆö ALL keys, grouped as in source

**Session settings:** `BeginString`, `SenderCompID`, `SenderSubID`, `SenderLocationID`, `TargetCompID`, `TargetSubID`, `TargetLocationID`, `SessionQualifier`, `DefaultApplVerID`, `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `Weekdays`, `TimeZone` (default UTC; IANA), `TimeStampPrecision` (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS, default MILLIS), `ResetOnLogon`, `RefreshOnLogon`, `ResetOnLogout`, `ResetOnDisconnect`, `ResetSeqTime` (HH:MM:SS, seqnum reset while staying connected).

**Validation settings:** `DataDictionary`, `TransportDataDictionary`, `AppDataDictionary` (supports `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4` prefix for per-ApplVerID dicts), `RejectInvalidMessage` (default Y), `AllowUnknownMsgFields` (const name; key string `ValidateUserDefinedFields`... actually key is `"AllowUnknownMsgFields"`), `CheckUserDefinedFields` (key string `"ValidateUserDefinedFields"`, default Y), `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder` (default Y), `ValidateFieldsHaveValues` (default Y), `CheckLatency` (default Y), `MaxLatency` (default 120), `InChanCapacity` (default 1; 0 = unbuffered).

**Initiator-only:** `ReconnectInterval` (default 30), `LogoutTimeout` (default 2), `LogonTimeout` (default 10), `HeartBtInt`, `SocketConnectHost` (+ `SocketConnectHost<n>` failover), `SocketConnectPort` (+ `SocketConnectPort<n>` failover), `SocketTimeout` (TLS handshake; go Duration), `ProxyType`, `ProxyHost`, `ProxyPort`, `ProxyUser`, `ProxyPassword`.

**Acceptor-only:** `SocketAcceptHost`, `SocketAcceptPort`, `HeartBtIntOverride` (default N), `UseTCPProxy` (HAProxy/ELB PROXY proto, default N), `DynamicSessions` (default N), `DynamicQualifier` (default N).

**Security/TLS:** `SocketPrivateKeyFile`, `SocketCertificateFile`, `SocketCAFile`, `SocketPrivateKeyBytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketCertificateBytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketCABytes` (raw PEM bytes), `SocketInsecureSkipVerify`, `SocketServerName` (SNI), `SocketMinimumTLSVersion` (SSL30/TLS10/TLS11/TLS12; default TLS12), `SocketUseSSL`.

**Logging:** `FileLogPath`, `SQLLogDriver`, `SQLLogDataSourceName`, `SQLLogConnMaxLifetime`, `MongoLogConnection`, `MongoLogDatabase`, `MongoLogReplicaSet`.

**Storage:** `PersistMessages` (default Y), `FileStorePath`, `FileStoreSync` (default Y), `SQLStoreDriver`, `SQLStoreDataSourceName`, `SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime`, `SQLStoreMessagesTableName` (default "messages"), `SQLStoreSessionsTableName` (default "sessions"), `MongoStoreConnection`, `MongoStoreDatabase`, `MongoStoreReplicaSet`.

**Misc:** `ResendRequestChunkSize` (default 0 = no chunking), `EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed` (tag 369), `EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum` (tag 789; not for <FIX.4.4).

## `session_factory.go` ŌĆö session options
Reads validator settings (ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder, ValidateFieldsHaveValues, RejectInvalidMessage, AllowUnknownMessageFields, CheckUserDefinedFields). Builds `Validator` (default w/o dictionaries; DataDictionary / TransportDataDictionary+AppDataDictionary for FIXT). FIXT requires `DefaultApplVerID` (maps BeginStringŌåÆApplVerID: FIX40=2ŌĆ”FIX5.0SP2=9). Reads ResetOnLogon/RefreshOnLogon/ResetOnLogout/ResetOnDisconnect/EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed/EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum/CheckLatency/MaxLatency/ResendRequestChunkSize. **StartTime/EndTime + TimeZone + StartDay/EndDay OR Weekdays** (mutually exclusive) ŌåÆ `internal.TimeRange`. **`ResetSeqTime`** ŌåÆ `s.EnableResetSeqTime=true`, `s.ResetSeqTime` parsed HH:MM:SS in TimeZone. **`TimeStampPrecision`** ŌåÆ Seconds/Millis/Micros/Nanos. **`InChanCapacity`** (default 1, must be Ōēź0). Initiator-only: ReconnectInterval (default 30s), LogoutTimeout (default 2s), LogonTimeout (default 10s), `configureSocketConnectAddress` collects all `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` into `session.SocketConnectAddress []string`. **`HeartBtIntOverride`**: acceptor uses config HeartBtInt only if override=Y; initiator always requires HeartBtInt.

## `acceptor.go`
`ConnectionValidator` interface: `Validate(netConn net.Conn, session SessionID) error` ŌĆö for custom auth (mTLS, IP whitelisting). `SetConnectionValidator`/`SetTLSConfig`/`SetNewListenerCallback`. **DynamicSessions**: creates sessions on-the-fly via `dynamicSessionsLoop` goroutine, registers/unregisters dynamically. **DynamicQualifier**: appends an incrementing counter as Qualifier to allow same SenderCompID. `UseTCPProxy` wraps listeners in `proxyproto.Listener` (HAProxy/ELB). Validates incoming logon BeginString/SenderCompID/TargetCompID (+Sub/Location), checks expected local port, runs connectionValidator, looks up session. Reads `SocketAcceptPort` per-session or global.

## `initiator.go` ŌĆö failover/multi-endpoint
**YES, failover/multi-endpoint**: `session.SocketConnectAddress` is a list; `address := session.SocketConnectAddress[connectionAttempt % len(...)]` cycles through all configured `SocketConnectHost/Port<n>` on each reconnect. Per-session goroutine; `waitForInSessionTime` (respects SessionTime) ŌåÆ dial via `proxy.ContextDialer` (SOCKS) ŌåÆ optional TLS handshake with auto-derived ServerName from address ŌåÆ `session.connect` + readLoop/writeLoop. Reconnect loop with `ReconnectInterval`, cancellable dial via context on stop.

## `dialer.go` ŌĆö SOCKS proxy
**YES, SOCKS5**: `ProxyType=socks` ŌåÆ `proxy.SOCKS5("tcp", host:port, proxyAuth, stdDialer)`. Supports `ProxyUser`/`ProxyPassword` auth. Falls back to plain `net.Dialer` if no ProxyType. `SocketTimeout` sets dialer timeout.

## `tls.go` ŌĆö TLS options
`loadTLSConfig`: `SocketUseSSL` (allow TLS w/o client certs), `SocketServerName`, `SocketInsecureSkipVerify`. Certs from files (`SocketPrivateKeyFile`+`SocketCertificateFile` via `tls.LoadX509KeyPair`) **OR raw PEM bytes** (`SocketPrivateKeyBytes`+`SocketCertificateBytes` via `tls.X509KeyPair`). CA from file (`SocketCAFile`) **or raw bytes** (`SocketCABytes`) ŌåÆ RootCAs+ClientCAs. Default `ClientAuth = RequireAndVerifyClientCert` (unless SocketUseSSL). **Min version** configurable (`setMinVersionExplicit`): SSL30/TLS10/TLS11/TLS12; default TLS12 via `defaultTLSConfig` (also PreferServerCipherSuites, CurveP256 only).

## Stores (`store/`): file, memory, mongo, sql
- **`store/file/file_store.go`**: **`FileStoreSync`** (default Y) controls `f.Sync()` on every write. Files: `.body`, `.header` (seqNum,offset,size CSV), `.session` (creation time), `.senderseqnums`, `.targetseqnums` (19-digit zero-padded). In-memory `MemoryStore` cache; `Refresh` repopulates. `IterateMessages` (streaming) + `GetMessages`. `SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum`. Respects `DynamicSessions`.
- **`store/sql/sql_store.go`**: generic multi-DB via any `database/sql` driver. **Postgres/pgx placeholder** support (`$1,$2` via `postgresPlaceholder`); other DBs use `?`. **Configurable table names** (`SQLStoreMessagesTableName` default "messages", `SQLStoreSessionsTableName` default "sessions"). Session key columns: beginstring, session_qualifier, sendercompid, sendersubid, senderlocid, targetcompid, targetsubid, targetlocid. `SQLStoreConnMaxLifetime`. `SaveMessageAndIncrNextSenderMsgSeqNum` uses a transaction. `IterateMessages` streaming.
- **memory**, **mongo** subdirs also present.

## Logs (`log/`): composite, file, mongo, screen, sql

## `internal/time_range.go` ŌĆö scheduling
`TimeRange` with `startTime`/`endTime` (TimeOfDay HH:MM:SS), `weekdays []time.Weekday`, optional `startDay`/`endDay *time.Weekday`, `loc *time.Location`. Constructors: `NewUTCTimeRange`, `NewTimeRangeInLocation`, `NewUTCWeekRange`, `NewWeekRangeInLocation`. `IsInRange(t)` handles daily, weekday-filtered, and week-spanning ranges (incl. overnight wrap). `IsInSameRange(t1,t2)` for session-boundary reset detection. Supports **StartDay/EndDay** (week-long sessions) and **Weekdays** (specific days of week) ŌĆö mutually exclusive.

## `_test/definitions/` ŌĆö 506 files
Top-level: `fields.fmt`, `lastseqnumprocessed`, `nextexpectedseqnum`, `resendreqchunksize`, `server/`. `server/` subdirs by FIX version: **fix40, fix41, fix42, fix43, fix44, fix50, fix50sp1, fix50sp2** (8 versions). Conformance/definition test specs.

## `cmd/generate-fix/` ŌĆö codegen CLI
`generate-fix.go`: takes data-dictionary XML path(s) arg. Auto-appends FIXT11 dict for FIX50/FIX50SP1/FIX50SP2. Generates (concurrently via goroutines+templates): `tag/tag_numbers.generated.go`, `field/fields.generated.go`, `enum/enums.generated.go`, per-package `header.generated.go`+`trailer.generated.go` (except fix50* which use fixt11), and per-message `<MsgName>.generated.go` under `fixPkg/<lowercaseMsgName>/`. Internal helpers: generate.go, globals.go, helpers.go, template_helpers.go, templates.go.

---

# 2. QuickFIX/J (`thrdpty/quickfixj/`) ŌĆö version 3.0.2-SNAPSHOT

## Modules
quickfixj-base, quickfixj-core, quickfixj-dictgenerator, quickfixj-orchestration, quickfixj-messages (fix40, fix41, fix42, fix43, fix44, fix50, fix50sp1, fix50sp2, **fixlatest**, fixt11, -all), quickfixj-examples, quickfixj-all, quickfixj-distribution, quickfixj-perf-test, quickfixj-stress-test. **FIX Latest module EXISTS** (`quickfixj-messages-fixlatest`).

## `docs/threading-model.md` ŌĆö threading strategies
Uses **Apache MINA** (NIO). Two `EventHandlingStrategy` impls:
- **SingleThreadedEventHandlingStrategy** ŌåÆ `SocketAcceptor`/`SocketInitiator`, single shared `BlockingQueue`, one daemon thread "QFJ Message Processor" calls `session.next(message)`.
- **ThreadPerSessionEventHandlingStrategy** ŌåÆ `ThreadedSocketAcceptor`/`ThreadedSocketInitiator`, per-session dispatcher thread "QF/J Session dispatcher: <sessionID>".
- **Timer thread** "QFJ Timer" (daemon, `ScheduledExecutorService` every 1000ms) iterates ALL sessions calling `Session.next()` (no-arg) for heartbeats/logon/schedule/timeouts ŌĆö orthogonal to message strategy.
Queue back-pressure: fixed-capacity OR watermark-based (`QueueTrackers.newMultiSessionWatermarkTracker` / `newSingleSessionWatermarkTracker`). Custom `Executor` injectable (e.g. Java 21 virtual threads). Thread-safety note: thread-per-session requires thread-safe `Application` if sharing state.

## OSGi support (`pom.xml`)
**YES**: `org.apache.felix:maven-bundle-plugin` (5.1.9) enabled in build (`<extensions>true</extensions>`, manifest goal execution). `skipBundlePlugin` profile to disable. Other deps: MINA 2.2.7, **slf4j 2.0.18** (`slf4j-jdk14`), Sleepycat `com.sleepycat:je` 18.3.12 (optional), dom4j 2.1.4 (dictgenerator), jaxen, Saxon, fix-orchestra 1.5.4/1.7.3, JAXB 4.0.5, commons-io, Netty 4.2.15. JDK 1.8 target. Maven 3.9.12.

## `MessageCracker.java` ŌĆö typed dispatch
Reflection-based `crack(Message, SessionID)`. **`@Handler` annotation** (METHOD, RUNTIME retention). Also supports naming convention `onMessage(Message, SessionID)`. Scans public methods with 2 params (Message subclass + SessionID), builds `Map<Class<?>, Invoker>`. `RedundantHandlerException` on duplicate. Fallback `onMessage` throws `UnsupportedMessageType`. Unwraps `InvocationTargetException`.

## `ApplicationFunctionalAdapter.java` & `ApplicationExtendedFunctionalAdapter.java`
Functional/lambda-style adapters. `ApplicationFunctionalAdapter` implements `Application`: CopyOnWriteArrayList listeners for onCreate/onLogon/onLogout; BiConsumer for toAdmin; custom `FromAdminListener`/`ToAppListener`/`FromAppListener` for fromAdmin/toApp/fromApp. **Type-safe listeners** keyed by Message class via `ConcurrentMap<Class, List>`. FIFO order; fail-fast exception propagation. `ApplicationExtendedFunctionalAdapter` extends it, implements `ApplicationExtended`: `canLogonPredicates` (Predicate<SessionID>, all-match/fail-fast) and `onBeforeSessionResetListeners` (Consumer<SessionID>).

## `JmxExporter.java`
JMX MBean export for connectors. Registration behavior constants: `REGISTRATION_FAIL_ON_EXISTING` (default), `REGISTRATION_IGNORE_EXISTING`, `REGISTRATION_REPLACE_EXISTING`. Uses platform MBeanServer (`ManagementFactory.getPlatformMBeanServer`) or creates one. `register(Connector)` delegates to `ConnectorJmxExporter`. Uses SLF4J logger. Spring-inspired MBeanRegistrationSupport design.

## `DoNotSend.java`, `RejectLogon.java`, `FieldNotFound.java`
- `DoNotSend` (Exception, base): thrown to abort sending/resending a message.
- `RejectLogon` (Exception): rejects logon with forced logout + immediate disconnect. Fields: `logoutBeforeDisconnect` (default true), `sessionStatus` (SessionStatus value, default -1). Multiple constructors.
- `FieldNotFound` (Exception): public `int field`; constructor `FieldNotFound(int field)`. Note about accessing fields in correct message section.

## Store types (`quickfixj-core/.../quickfix/`)
**FileStore**, **CachedFileStore** (+ CachedFileStoreFactory), **MemoryStore** (+ MemoryStoreFactory), **JdbcStore** (+ JdbcStoreFactory, JdbcUtil, JdbcSetting, JdbcLog), **SleepycatStore** (+ SleepycatStoreFactory, Berkeley DB JE), plus **NoopStore** (+ NoopStoreFactory), InMemoryMessageQueue (+ BoundInMemoryMessageQueue), MessageStore/MessageStoreFactory interfaces.

## `FileStore.java` ŌĆö syncWrites, maxCachedMsgs, ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore
Constructor `FileStore(path, sessionID, syncWrites, maxCachedMsgs)`. **`syncWrites`**: when true, opens files with mode `"rwd"` (RandomAccessFile SYNC_OPTION 'd') and calls `headerFileOutputStream.getFD().sync()` after each header write. **`maxCachedMsgs`**: when >0, maintains in-memory `TreeMap<Long,long[]> messageIndex` (seqNumŌåÆ[offset,size]) capped at maxCachedMsgs (evicts lowest via `pollFirstEntry`); when 0, index is null (always reads header file). Files: `.body`, `.header` (binary: int seqNum, long offset, int size), `.senderseqnums`, `.targetseqnums` (writeUTF), `.session` (creation time). `FileStoreFactory`: `SETTING_FILE_STORE_MAX_CACHED_MSGS = "FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs"` (default 10000), `SETTING_FILE_STORE_SYNC`. **`ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore`** is a Session setting (not FileStore) ŌĆö see config below.

## Config options (from `Session.java` grep) ŌĆö ALL confirmed present
`CheckCompID` (`SETTING_CHECK_COMP_ID`), `NonStopSession` (`SETTING_NON_STOP_SESSION`, in DefaultSessionSchedule), `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder`, `ValidateUnorderedGroupFields`, `FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter`, `RejectInvalidMessage`, `RejectMessageOnUnhandledException` (`SETTING_REJECT_MESSAGE_ON_UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION`), `ResetOnError`, `DisconnectOnError`, `DisableHeartBeatCheck`, `SendRedundantResendRequests`, `ClosedResendInterval` (`SETTING_USE_CLOSED_RESEND_INTERVAL`), `ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore`, `MaxScheduledWriteRequests` (`SETTING_MAX_SCHEDULED_WRITE_REQUESTS`), `LogonTag` (`SETTING_LOGON_TAG` ŌĆö tag=value pairs, e.g. LogonTag=553=user, LogonTag1=554=password; List<StringField>), `TimeStampPrecision` (`SETTING_TIMESTAMP_PRECISION`, UtcTimestampPrecision enum, MILLIS default, SECONDS for <FIX.4.2), `ContinueInitializationOnError` (`SETTING_CONTINUE_INIT_ON_ERROR` in SessionFactory). Session constructor also takes: checkLatency/maxLatency, refreshOnLogon, checkCompID, logonIntervals, allowedRemoteAddresses (Set<InetAddress>), requiresOrigSendingTime, validateChecksum, heartBeatTimeoutMultiplier, rejectGarbledMessage. `DefaultSessionSchedule`/`SessionSchedule` interface has `isNonStopSession()`.

## SLF4J integration
**YES**: slf4j 2.0.18, `slf4j-jdk14` binding in dependencyManagement. JmxExporter uses `org.slf4j.Logger`. Pervasive logging via SLF4J throughout.

## `dictgenerator/Generator.java`
`org.quickfixj.dictgenerator.Generator` (QFJ-483). Takes FPL 2008/09 repository dir + major/minor. `generate()` produces `session.xml` (admin), `application.xml`, and `merged.xml` (for <FIX5.0). Uses dom4j. Note: only works with FPL 2008/09 repository files. (FIX Latest generation uses the separate orchestra tools / quickfixj-orchestration module + `quickfixj-from-fix-orchestra-*-maven-plugin`.)

## Examples (`quickfixj-examples/`)
- **executor**: `Application.java`, `Executor.java`, `MarketDataProvider.java`.
- **banzai**: `Banzai.java`, `BanzaiApplication.java`, `Order.java`, `OrderTableModel.java`, `ExecutionTableModel.java`, `OrderSide/OrderTIF/OrderType`, `MarketDataProvider`, `ui/` (Swing GUI), `DoubleNumberTextField`/`IntegerNumberTextField`, `LogonEvent`, `TwoWayMap`.
- **ordermatch**: `Application.java`, `Main.java`, `Market.java`, `Order.java`, `OrderMatcher.java`, `IdGenerator.java`.

---

## Cross-cutting comparison notes
- Both support FIX 4.0ŌĆō4.4, 5.0/SP1/SP2, FIXT1.1; QFJ additionally has **FIX Latest** module (Go does not).
- Go stores: file/memory/sql/mongo. QFJ stores: file/cachedfile/memory/jdbc/sleepycat(Berkeley)/noop + in-memory message queues.
- Both have file-store sync option (Go `FileStoreSync`, QFJ `syncWrites`/`FileStoreMaxCachedMsgs`).
- Both support TLS (Go richer raw-bytes+min-version; QFJ via MINA SSL), SOCKS proxy (Go only via `golang.org/x/net/proxy`), TCP PROXY proto (Go `UseTCPProxy`).
- Both support multi-endpoint failover (Go `SocketConnectHost<n>` modulo cycling; QFJ `SocketConnectHost<n>` via initiator reconnect).
- Go uses config-driven validation; QFJ has richer session-level knobs (NonStopSession, ResetOnError, DisconnectOnError, DisableHeartBeatCheck, SendRedundantResendRequests, ClosedResendInterval, ForceResendWhenCorruptedStore, RejectMessageOnUnhandledException, MaxScheduledWriteRequests, LogonTag, CheckCompID, ValidateUnorderedGroupFields, FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter, ContinueInitializationOnError, heartBeatTimeoutMultiplier, allowedRemoteAddresses, validateChecksum).
- QFJ: OSGi bundles, SLF4J, JMX, MINA NIO, functional Application adapters, `@Handler` annotation cracking, dynamic FIX Latest codegen via Orchestra.

Next steps:
The inventory is complete for all requested items. Both implementations were read from actual source. No remaining gaps on the requested checklist. If deeper detail is needed on a specific item (e.g. full QFJ Session.java constructor signature list, JdbcStore SQL schema, Go datadictionary validator internals, or the orchestration/FIX-Latest codegen pipeline), those files can be opened next ŌĆö but every explicitly-requested file and feature flag has been located and documented.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once ŌĆö it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

functionDeclarations.name: complete_task
functionDeclarations.description:
Call this tool to submit your final result and complete the task. This is the ONLY way to finish.

This tool must be called ALONE in its turn. Do not call any other tools in the same turn. If you still need another tool, do not call this yet.

Your result MUST be an informative handoff for the caller (main agent), not a one-line summary.

Requirements for result:
- Use 2+ short paragraphs or a short markdown sectioned report
- Include what you did / found and the concrete evidence that supports it
- Include file paths, errors, command outcomes, or other artifacts when relevant
- Do NOT return only a title or a single-line summary
- Keep it concise, but detailed enough that the main agent can act without reopening your full trace
- Choose a status that matches the outcome: use 'success' for fully completed work, 'partial' for meaningful progress with straightforward continuation, 'blocked' for external blockers that you cannot resolve autonomously, and 'failure' for invalid or unrecoverable paths

Good result example:
## Findings
I inspected `packages/core/src/foo.ts` and `packages/cli/src/bar.ts`.
The bug is caused by `baz()` skipping null checks in the headless path.

## Evidence
- `packages/core/src/foo.ts:42` calls `baz(input!)`
- `npm test -- foo` fails with `TypeError: ...`

## Next steps
Update the null guard in `foo.ts` and rerun the focused test.

Good result example (partial completion due to budget):
## Completed
- Inspected `packages/core/src/foo.ts` and confirmed the bug location
- Ran `npm test -- foo` to reproduce the failure

## Findings
- The root cause is a missing null guard in `baz()` at line 42
- The fix requires updating both `foo.ts` and the corresponding test file

## Remaining
- Actual code fix not applied (budget exhausted before edit)
- Test update for the new null guard path

## Blockers
Budget exhausted. The fix is straightforward ŌĆö apply the null guard and rerun tests.

Bad result example:
## Findings
Investigated the issue.

result.description: Provide a substantive handoff of what you did / found. Do not return only a title or single-line summary; include enough evidence and detail for the main agent to act on directly.

next_steps.description: Actionable next steps, or 'none' if there are no follow-ups.

# 1. QuickFIX/Go (`thrdpty/quickfix/`)

## Config keys (`config/configuration.go`) ŌĆö ALL keys, grouped as in source

**Session settings:** `BeginString`, `SenderCompID`, `SenderSubID`, `SenderLocationID`, `TargetCompID`, `TargetSubID`, `TargetLocationID`, `SessionQualifier`, `DefaultApplVerID`, `StartTime`, `EndTime`, `StartDay`, `EndDay`, `Weekdays`, `TimeZone` (default UTC; IANA), `TimeStampPrecision` (SECONDS/MILLIS/MICROS/NANOS, default MILLIS), `ResetOnLogon`, `RefreshOnLogon`, `ResetOnLogout`, `ResetOnDisconnect`, `ResetSeqTime` (HH:MM:SS, seqnum reset while staying connected).

**Validation settings:** `DataDictionary`, `TransportDataDictionary`, `AppDataDictionary` (supports `AppDataDictionary.FIX.4.4` prefix for per-ApplVerID dicts), `RejectInvalidMessage` (default Y), `AllowUnknownMsgFields` (const name; key string `ValidateUserDefinedFields`... actually key is `"AllowUnknownMsgFields"`), `CheckUserDefinedFields` (key string `"ValidateUserDefinedFields"`, default Y), `ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder` (default Y), `ValidateFieldsHaveValues` (default Y), `CheckLatency` (default Y), `MaxLatency` (default 120), `InChanCapacity` (default 1; 0 = unbuffered).

**Initiator-only:** `ReconnectInterval` (default 30), `LogoutTimeout` (default 2), `LogonTimeout` (default 10), `HeartBtInt`, `SocketConnectHost` (+ `SocketConnectHost<n>` failover), `SocketConnectPort` (+ `SocketConnectPort<n>` failover), `SocketTimeout` (TLS handshake; go Duration), `ProxyType`, `ProxyHost`, `ProxyPort`, `ProxyUser`, `ProxyPassword`.

**Acceptor-only:** `SocketAcceptHost`, `SocketAcceptPort`, `HeartBtIntOverride` (default N), `UseTCPProxy` (HAProxy/ELB PROXY proto, default N), `DynamicSessions` (default N), `DynamicQualifier` (default N).

**Security/TLS:** `SocketPrivateKey
